title: POST-H-018-D — Policy/approval/RBAC binding para conectores
commands: python -m devpilot_core connector sandbox run --mode replay --json --write-report

python -m pytest -p no:ddtrace tests/test_post_h_018_connector_policy_binding.py tests/test_post_h_018_connector_replay.py tests/test_post_h_018_connector_sandbox_runner.py tests/test_post_h_018_connector_sandbox_policy.py tests/test_schema_registry.py tests/test_project_global_state.py tests/test_post_h_006_e_cli_no_growth_gate.py -q
evidence: ConnectorPolicyExposureReport lists connectors by risk and proves connector.write_future blocked.
Side-effecting connector classifications require ApprovalPolicyChecker and block without approval.
Sandbox replay remains local fixture-backed with redaction checks.
limitations: implemented-initial; no connector write is enabled.
No real external connector integration, OAuth, API keys, webhooks or network calls are implemented.
Final quality gate and connector sandbox runbook remain POST-H-018-E scope.
pass_criteria: Every connector has Policy/Approval/RBAC binding coverage.
connector.write_future is evaluated and blocked for every connector.
Read-only connectors keep policy coverage and safe replay/dry-run behavior.
High and critical risk connectors evaluate RBAC.
The exposure report lists connectors by risk with blocking_findings_total=0.
block_criteria: connector write can pass without approval.
A high or critical risk connector skips RBAC evaluation.
Any report declares network, external API, mutation, connector write, remote execution or plugin execution as used.
risks: implemented-initial; no connector write is enabled.
Approval/RBAC binding is deterministic and local; it does not authorize production external integrations.
Final connector-sandbox quality gate and operational runbook remain POST-H-018-E scope.
